revision.text:
[[arz:ايواما دوجو]][[cs:Ibaraki dódžo]][[en:Iwama Dōjō]][[fr:Iwama dojo]][[hr:Iwama dođo]][[ja:合氣神社]][[pl:Aiki-jinja]][[vi:Iwama Dōjō]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Aiki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Aiki Jinja.JPG | location = 〒319-0203
270-4 Yoshioka, Kasama City, Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan | LatitudeDeg = 36 | LatitudeMin = 17 | LatitudeSec = 39.2 | LongitudeDeg = 140 | LongitudeMin = 16 | LongitudeSec = 52.9 | Enshrineddeity = Susanoo no Ōkami | SacredObject = | ShrineRank = | consecration_year = | MainHall = | Pilgrimage = | ChiefPriest = | Othernames = | AnnualFestival = April 29 | ReligiousRites = | locationMap = Japan Ibaraki | location = {{ill|Iwama|qid=Q1011725|1=Iwama, Ibaraki}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.294222222222224|N|140.28136111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Aiki Shrine''' (合氣神社, Aiki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Kasama City]], {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, Japan. It was established by {{ill|Morihei Ueshiba|en|Morihei Ueshiba|qid=Q182130|lt=Morihei Ueshiba|1=Morihei Ueshiba}}, the founder of {{ill|Aikido|en|Aikido|qid=Q43114|lt=Aikido|1=Aikido}}, and is the only shrine dedicated to Aikido. == Overview == In {{ill|1942|en|1942|qid=Q18625|lt=1942|1=1942}} (Showa 17), Morihei Ueshiba relocated with his wife Hatsu from Tokyo to the former Iwama Town (now part of Kasama City). He built a 36-tatami mat dojo and a shrine on the site. Here, Ueshiba pursued a lifestyle blending martial arts and farming, perfecting Aikido as the 'martial art of harmony.' Ueshiba himself referred to the site as the "birthplace" ({{nihongo|産屋|ubuyashi|qq=}}) of Aikido and regarded it as sacred. During this time, {{ill|Morihiro Saito|en|Morihiro Saito|qid=Q550626|lt=Morihiro Saito|1=Morihiro Saito}} was one of the close disciples serving Ueshiba. After Ueshiba's death, Saito continued to protect Aiki Shrine and passed on Ueshiba's later techniques, which became known as the [[Iwama-ryu]] style. In {{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}} (Heisei 13), the third Doshu, {{ill|Moriteru Ueshiba|en|Moriteru Ueshiba|qid=Q382028|lt=Moriteru Ueshiba|1=Moriteru Ueshiba}}, and Morihiro Saito restored the shrine and erected a commemorative stone monument. The inscription on the monument, "Aiki Shrine," was written by {{ill|Seiseki Abe|en|Seiseki Abe|qid=Q3267572|lt=Seiseki Abe|1=Seiseki Abe}}, a disciple of Ueshiba and a renowned calligrapher. In {{ill|2009|en|2009|qid=Q1996|lt=2009|1=2009}} (Heisei 21), a bronze statue of {{ill|Morihei Ueshiba|en|Morihei Ueshiba|qid=Q182130|lt=Morihei Ueshiba|1=Morihei Ueshiba}} was installed in the shrine grounds by a committee led by {{ill|Hiroshi Isoyama|en|Hiroshi Isoyama|qid=Q1620278|lt=Hiroshi Isoyama|1=Hiroshi Isoyama}}.{{Cite web |url=http://www13.big.or.jp/~aikikai/new2009.html |title=Unveiling and Commemoration Ceremony of the Statue of Aikido Founder Morihei Ueshiba |work=WHAT'S NEW |publisher=Ibaraki Branch Dojo, Aikikai Foundation |date=2009-12-08 |access-date=2016-12-08|qq=}} Currently, Aiki Shrine is owned by the Ueshiba family and managed by the Ibaraki Branch Dojo of the {{ill|Aikikai|en|Aikikai|qid=Q1572931|lt=Aikikai|1=Aikikai}}. == Enshrined Deities == According to the shrine's origin records, the following deities are enshrined: * Susanoo no Ōkami * Takemikazuchi no Ōkami * Futsunushi no Ōkami * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami]] * Kunitsu Ryūō * Kuzuryū Daigongen * [[Tajikarao no Mikoto]] * Amanomurakumo Kuki Samuhara Ryūō * Ietsumi Mikoto * [[Wakumusubi no Mikoto]] * Other deities including Ryūō, Daigongen, Great Tengu, Great Bodhisattvas (43 deities in total) == festival == The annual festival (例大祭) is held on {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|qid=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}} and features dedication demonstrations organized by Aikikai. Following the passing of the second Doshu, [[:en:Kisshōmaru Ueshiba|Kisshōmaru Ueshiba]], a memorial service for the founder is also conducted. The event draws not only members of the Aikikai and individuals associated with successive Doshu but also many Aikido practitioners from overseas. Over 1,500 people visit Aiki Shrine on this day alone. == References == {{Footnote Help|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www13.big.or.jp/~aikikai/index.html Aikikai Foundation Ibaraki Branch Dojo] * {{Wayback |url=http://www.city.kasama.lg.jp/kankou/05taiken/cnt/aikido.html |title=Aikido |date=20110719092856|qq=}} - Kasama City {{translated page|ja|合氣神社|version=103124619|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:合氣神社]] [[ja:合気神社]] [[arz:ايواما دوجو]] [[cs:Ibaraki dódžo]] [[en:Iwama Dōjō]] [[fr:Iwama dojo]] [[hr:Iwama dođo]] [[pl:Aiki-jinja]] [[vi:Iwama Dōjō]] {{wikidata link|Q2827917}} [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Aikido]] [[Category:笠間市の建築物]] [[Category:笠間市の歴史]]
